# Beudox HR - Technical Context for AI Assistants

## Architecture Overview

Beudox HR is a React-based single-page application (SPA) built with TypeScript and Vite. It follows a component-based architecture with clear separation of concerns:

- **Frontend Framework**: React 18 with functional components and hooks
- **Routing**: React Router DOM with protected routes and role-based access control
- **State Management**: React Query for server state, local component state for UI
- **Styling**: Tailwind CSS with shadcn/ui design system
- **Backend**: Supabase (PostgreSQL database + Auth + Edge Functions)

## Key Architectural Patterns

### Component Organization
- `src/components/ui/` - Reusable UI primitives (buttons, forms, dialogs, etc.)
- `src/components/layout/` - Layout components (AppLayout, Sidebar, TopBar)
- `src/components/[feature]/` - Feature-specific components
- `src/pages/` - Page-level components mapped to routes

### Data Flow
- **Authentication**: Supabase Auth with custom AuthProvider context
- **API Calls**: React Query hooks for data fetching and mutations
- **Forms**: React Hook Form with Zod schema validation
- **Real-time**: Supabase real-time subscriptions where needed

### Role-Based Access Control
- **Roles**: hr_manager, ceo, team_lead, employee
- **Implementation**: `canAccess()` function checks role against route patterns
- **Protection**: ProtectedRoute component wraps authenticated pages

## Database Schema & Relationships

### Core Tables
- `employees` - User profiles with role assignments
- `companies` - Multi-tenant company data
- `projects` - Project management with team assignments
- `clients` - Client information linked to projects
- `invoices` - Invoice generation and tracking
- `evaluations` - Quarterly performance reviews
- `daily_evaluations` - Daily feedback system (peer/team reviews)
- `hr_policies` - Company policies with rich text content
- `payroll` - Automated payroll calculations
- `loans` - Employee loan management

### Key Relationships
- Employees belong to companies
- Projects have many employees (many-to-many)
- Evaluations link employees to evaluators
- Daily evaluations support peer reviews
- Invoices connect projects, clients, and employees

## Key Files & Their Purposes

### Entry Points
- `src/main.tsx` - Application bootstrap
- `src/App.tsx` - Main app component with routing

### Core Components
- `src/components/layout/AppLayout.tsx` - Main layout wrapper
- `src/components/layout/AppSidebar.tsx` - Navigation sidebar
- `src/components/layout/TopBar.tsx` - Top navigation bar
- `src/components/BeudoxLogo.tsx` - Logo component with variants
- `src/components/SearchableEmployeeSelect.tsx` - Employee selection dropdown
- `src/components/evaluations/EvaluationTimeline.tsx` - Evaluation history display
- `src/components/hr-policies/RichTextEditor.tsx` - Rich text editor for policies

### Hooks & Utilities
- `src/hooks/useAuth.ts` - Authentication state management
- `src/lib/role-access.ts` - Role-based access control logic
- `src/lib/utils.ts` - Utility functions (cn for class merging)
- `src/integrations/supabase/` - Supabase client and type definitions

### Configuration
- `vite.config.ts` - Build configuration
- `tailwind.config.ts` - Styling configuration
- `tsconfig.json` - TypeScript configuration
- `supabase/config.toml` - Supabase project configuration

## Business Logic Patterns

### Evaluation System
- **Quarterly Evaluations**: Formal performance reviews with recommendations
- **Daily Evaluations**: Quick feedback system with direction (positive/constructive)
- **Visibility Rules**: Managers see all, team leads see relevant, employees see their own

### Payroll System
- **Automated Generation**: Based on attendance, overtime, allowances
- **PDF Generation**: Server-side PDF creation for payslips
- **Role Restrictions**: HR managers and CEOs can generate payroll

### Invoice Management
- **Project-Based**: Invoices linked to projects and clients
- **PDF Export**: Automated PDF generation with company branding
- **Status Tracking**: Draft, sent, paid status management

## Development Workflow

### Scripts
- `npm run dev` - Development server
- `npm run build` - Production build
- `npm run test` - Unit tests (Vitest)
- `npm run lint` - Code linting
- `npx playwright test` - E2E tests

### Code Quality
- **TypeScript**: Strict type checking
- **ESLint**: Code linting with React rules
- **Prettier**: Code formatting (via ESLint)
- **Testing**: Unit tests with Vitest, E2E with Playwright

## Deployment Considerations

- **Static Hosting**: Built as static SPA, deployable to Netlify, Vercel, etc.
- **Environment Variables**: Supabase credentials via VITE_ prefixed env vars
- **Database Migrations**: Supabase migrations in `supabase/migrations/`
- **Edge Functions**: Serverless functions for PDF generation, email sending

## Common Development Tasks

### Adding a New Page
1. Create component in `src/pages/`
2. Add route in `src/App.tsx` with appropriate protection
3. Update navigation in `AppSidebar.tsx` if needed

### Adding a New Feature Component
1. Create component in appropriate `src/components/` subdirectory
2. Export from component index if needed
3. Use in pages or other components

### Database Changes
1. Create migration in `supabase/migrations/`
2. Update TypeScript types in `src/integrations/supabase/types.ts`
3. Test with local Supabase instance

### Adding New UI Components
1. Use shadcn/ui CLI to add components: `npx shadcn-ui@latest add [component]`
2. Customize in `src/components/ui/`
3. Follow existing patterns for consistency
